heading: Conveyance by President to people of lands, buildings, etc.
label: 748 Conveyance by President to people of lands, buildings, etc.
text: The President may, from time to time, in his discretion, convey to the people of Puerto Rico, such lands, buildings, or interests in lands, or other property now owned by the United States, and within the territorial limits of Puerto Rico as in his opinion are no longer needed for purposes of the United States. And he may from time to time accept by legislative grant from Puerto Rico any lands, buildings, or other interests or property which may be needed for public purposes by the United States.
notes: CodificationSection is comprised of proviso clause of section 7 of act . The text preceding the proviso clause of section 7 is classified to .Mar. 2, 1917section 747 of this title
Change of Name“Puerto Rico” substituted in text for “Porto Rico” pursuant to act , which is classified to .May 17, 1932section 731a of this title
